calculation.method: reported close and daily move from the AP markets wire

text: The S&P 500 fell 91.25 points, or 1.2%, to 7,420.10 in the June 17, 2026 US regular session.

calculation.method: reported close and daily move from the AP markets wire; the AP noted the Dow swung from a 0.5% morning gain to a 1% loss

text: The Dow Jones Industrial Average fell 507.12 points, or 1%, to 51,492.55 in the June 17, 2026 US regular session, after giving up an early 0.5% gain.

calculation.method: reported close and daily move from the AP markets wire

text: The Nasdaq Composite fell 354.69 points, or 1.3%, to 26,021.66 in the June 17, 2026 US regular session.

calculation.method: reported close and daily move from the AP markets wire

text: The Russell 2000 fell 21.21 points, or 0.7%, to 2,917.98 in the June 17, 2026 US regular session.

market_session: US regular session close; AP week-to-date basis

calculation.method: reported week-to-date point and percent changes from the AP markets wire

text: For the week through the June 17 close, the AP reported the S&P 500 down 11.36 points, or 0.2%; the Dow up 290.29 points, or 0.6%; the Nasdaq up 132.81 points, or 0.5%; and the Russell 2000 down 26.01 points, or 0.9%.

market_session: US regular session close; AP year-to-date basis

calculation.method: reported year-to-date point and percent changes from the AP markets wire

text: For the year through the June 17 close, the AP reported the S&P 500 up 574.60 points, or 8.4%; the Dow up 3,429.26 points, or 7.1%; the Nasdaq up 2,779.67 points, or 12%; and the Russell 2000 up 436.07 points, or 17.6%.

market_session: FOMC June 16-17, 2026 meeting; statement released 2:00 p.m. EDT 2026-06-17
text: The FOMC kept the target range for the federal funds rate at 3-1/2 to 3-3/4 percent (3.50%-3.75%) at its June 16-17, 2026 meeting and reaffirmed its policy of maintaining ample reserves.

market_session: FOMC June 16-17, 2026 meeting; statement released 2:00 p.m. EDT 2026-06-17
text: The FOMC approved the June 17, 2026 statement by a unanimous 12-0 vote.

The FOMC statement said inflation remains elevated relative to the Committee's 2 percent goal, in part reflecting supply shocks that have driven price increases in certain sectors including energy, and pledged to deliver price stability while describing economic activity as expanding at a solid pace despite elevated uncertainty tied in part to the Middle East conflict.

market_session: FOMC June 2026 Summary of Economic Projections

calculation.method: June 2026 SEP median midpoint of the appropriate end-2026 federal funds target range versus the March 2026 SEP median

text: The June 2026 Summary of Economic Projections raised the median projected appropriate federal funds rate for end-2026 to 3.8%, up from 3.4% in the March 2026 projections, a hawkish shift in the dot plot.

market_session: FOMC June 2026 Summary of Economic Projections

calculation.method: count of 2026 dot-plot midpoints at or above 3.875% (one 25 bp hike above the current 3.625% midpoint): 3 at 3.875%, 5 at 4.125%, 1 at 4.375%

text: In the June 2026 dot plot, 9 of 18 participants placed the appropriate end-2026 federal funds midpoint at or above 3.875%, implying at least one quarter-point hike from the current 3.625% midpoint; the AP characterized this as nearly half of policymakers foreseeing at least one increase this year.

market_session: FOMC June 2026 Summary of Economic Projections

calculation.method: June 2026 SEP median headline and core PCE inflation projections for 2026 versus the March 2026 SEP medians

text: The June 2026 SEP lifted the median 2026 PCE inflation projection to 3.6% from 2.7% in March, and the median 2026 core PCE projection to 3.3% from 2.7%, consistent with the statement's energy supply-shock language.
The AP reported Treasury yields climbed on rising expectations for a rate hike in 2026 after the Fed's projections; higher rates can slow inflation but also weigh on the economy and asset prices.

subject.institution: Board of Governors of the Federal Reserve System

market_session: FOMC implementation note issued 2:00 p.m. EDT 2026-06-17
text: The June 17, 2026 implementation note kept the interest rate on reserve balances at 3.65% effective June 18, 2026, with the standing repo rate at 3.75%, the overnight reverse repo offering rate at 3.5% (per-counterparty limit $160 billion), and the primary credit rate at 3.75%.
